raw_consumer_statement:
I was traveling from XXXX XXXX, FL to XXXX, MA to attend the XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ). Concurrently, I was going to attend 2 of my son 's college soccer games, and visit my daughter who lives in XXXX. I made reservations months ahead at the XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX, MA. Upon my arrival at XXXX XXXX in XXXX, MA, I called the hotel to let them know we ( my wife and I ) were on our way. I was told that I no longer had a reservation. I demanded to speak with the manager but was denied and the person hung up on me. We then spent the next 6 hours driving and calling around all of XXXX, XXXX MA, and XXXX, RI and EVERY hotel was SOLD OUT. Apparently ALL of Massachusetts and Rhode Island was sold out because of the XXXX conference, travel softball and soccer games. Thus, we had nowhere to stay. We stayed that one night at my daughter 's XXXX apartment and returned to XXXX XXXX the next day. I was unable to attend the conference, see my son 's 2 soccer games, or visit with my daughter. Upon arrival home, I notified American Express who told me to wait to view my monthly statement to see if I was billed for the hotel. When the statement arrived, I was billed more than {$2000.00}. I called AMEX and they reviewed my appeal and told me pretty much, " too bad/too sad '' there was nothing they could do. As of now, I have appealed their decision 3 times and all 3 times they have denied any responsibility and have not refunded my money. I find this egregious ( especially as I have been an AMEX card holder for more than 30 years. I am requesting your assistance in finding out why given the circumstances of my case, 1 ) why they rejected my request, 2 ) why are they billing me for all those nights stay when I did not stay even 1 night, and 3 ) why they rejected my request when they know I was not able to attend my conference ( thus denied the ability to receive continuing education credit to renew my professional license, not attend my son 's soccer games, or visit with my daughter ). Pardon my vernacular but I do not accept that I have to pay more than {$2000.00} to get completely XXXX. 
Thank you for your assistance, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX  XXXX
